query: Lee Jae-myung approval rating Gallup Korea Realmeter late June early July 2026, is approval still falling, what is driving the decline, any dead cross
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Lee Jae-myung's approval ratings in late June/early July 2026:

**Is approval still falling?** No — it stopped falling and modestly recovered after bottoming around **June 26**. Both pollsters show a late-June trough followed by a July rebound.

**Gallup Korea:** Approval hit **51% (June 24–26)**, the lowest of his presidency, then recovered to **54%** (June 30–July 2) and held at **53%** (July 7–9). Gallup **never showed a dead cross** — approval stayed above disapproval throughout.

**Realmeter:** A genuine **dead cross DID occur** — beginning June 15–19 (46.7% approve vs 49.7% disapprove), the first since his inauguration. It lasted ~3 weeks before he regained a **"golden cross"** in the July 6–10 survey (48.9% vs 47.7%).

**What drove the decline:** Economy/livelihood concerns and high exchange rates (고환율), stock-market slump, real estate/housing policy (46% "bad" vs 26% "good"), "excessive" welfare spending, and unilateral-governance perceptions.

**What drove the rebound:** The government's "three mega-projects" (regional investment announcements) and diplomatic wins (NATO summit, Korea-Mongolia summit, defense-export gains).

**Key nuance:** The dead cross appeared only in Realmeter because its daily tracker ran ~5–7pp below Gallup during this window. Analysts also flagged a "decoupling" — Lee's personal number recovered while Democratic Party support stayed flat in early July.

key_facts: Gallup Korea: Lee's approval hit 51% on June 24-26, 2026 — the lowest since he took office in June 2025 (disapproval ~41%, approval down 6pp).
Gallup: approval rebounded to 54% (June 30-July 2, disapproval 36%) then held at 53% (July 7-9, disapproval 35%) — decline stopped, no dead cross in Gallup.
Realmeter DID record a genuine 'dead cross' (disapproval > approval): began June 15-19 (approve 46.7% vs disapprove 49.7%), the first since inauguration.
Realmeter dead cross lasted ~3 consecutive weeks (June wk3, June 22-23, and June 29-July 3 at 47.0% vs 49.2%).
Realmeter 'golden cross' recovery in July 6-10 survey: approve 48.9% (+1.9pp) vs disapprove 47.7% (-1.5pp) — approval led for first time in 4 weeks.
Realmeter approval peaked at 60.5% in May week 2, then fell for 6 straight weeks before the early-July rebound.
Top negative drivers: economy/livelihood + high exchange rates (고환율), real estate policy (46% bad vs 26% good in Gallup), 'excessive' welfare, and unilateral governance perceptions.
Rebound drivers: government's 'three mega-projects' regional investment announcements; diplomatic wins (NATO summit, Korea-Mongolia summit, defense-export gains).
Party support Realmeter July wk2: Democratic Party 44.8% vs People Power Party 38.1% (gap 6.7pp, outside margin of error); Gallup early July DPK 41% vs PPP 26%.
Decoupling noted: Lee's personal approval recovered in early July while DPK party support stayed flat.
Realmeter's daily tracker ran ~5-7pp below Gallup during this period, explaining why only Realmeter showed a dead cross.

data_points.metric: Gallup real estate policy negative rating
